meta.name: Causa Rellena
meta.subtitle: Chilled layered mashed potato terrine with ají amarillo, lime, and savory filling

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalogs
notes: Canonical home method for causa: yellow potato base seasoned with ají amarillo + lime, layered with tuna/chicken/avocado filling.
Designed for deterministic adaptation: mayo/yogurt swap, tuna/chicken/vegetarian fillings, gluten-free by default.

summary: Causa rellena is a chilled Peruvian layered dish: ají amarillo–lime mashed potatoes wrapped around a creamy filling (tuna or chicken), often layered with avocado and topped with egg, olives, and herbs.

yield.notes: Serves 6 as a lunch/main or 8–10 as an appetizer.

signals.weeknightPossible: Yes, especially with canned tuna or leftover chicken.

heat.note: Heat depends on ají amarillo paste. Causa is usually mildly spicy.

optional.label: Piping bag
optional.notes: Nice for clean layers and decorative topping.

notes: Tuna filling contains fish; mayo and egg garnish contain egg.
Dairy is optional (some versions add butter).

ingredients.label: Yellow potatoes (Yukon gold preferred), peeled
ingredients.notes: Peruvian yellow potatoes are traditional; Yukon gold works very well.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Includes salting the potato water and seasoning the mash.

ingredients.label: Ají amarillo paste
ingredients.notes: Start at 1 tbsp for mild; increase to 3 tbsp for a stronger ají presence.

ingredients.label: Fresh lime juice
ingredients.notes: Key flavor. Add gradually and taste as you go.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil (or mild olive oil)
ingredients.notes: Helps create a smooth, cohesive potato layer.

ingredients.label: Canned tuna, drained
ingredients.notes: Two 6-oz cans. Substitute shredded chicken for a 'causa de pollo'.

ingredients.label: Mayonnaise
ingredients.notes: Adjust for creaminess. Can swap part with Greek yogurt.

ingredients.label: Red onion, very finely diced
ingredients.notes: Optional but common; soak in cold water 5 minutes to mellow.

ingredients.label: Ají amarillo paste (for filling)
ingredients.notes: Optional—keeps filling aligned with the potato base flavor.

ingredients.label: Avocados, sliced
ingredients.notes: Classic layer. Toss lightly with lime to prevent browning.

steps.title: Boil potatoes until tender
instructions: Cut potatoes into large chunks for even cooking.
Place in a pot, cover with cold water, and add 2 tsp salt.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a steady simmer until potatoes are very tender.
Drain thoroughly and let steam-dry 3–5 minutes to remove excess moisture.
checks: Potatoes mash easily with no firm centers.
Excess water is steamed off to prevent a watery causa.

steps.title: Mash potatoes and season with ají + lime
instructions: Mash hot potatoes until smooth (a ricer yields the silkiest texture).
While still warm, mix in ají amarillo paste, lime juice, and oil.
Season with salt to taste (start with remaining 1/2 tsp, then adjust). Add black pepper if desired.
Mix until cohesive and smooth. Cool the potato mixture until just warm (easy to handle).
checks: Potato base is smooth and cohesive, not gluey.
Flavor is balanced: salty + bright lime + fruity ají.

steps.title: Mix the filling
instructions: In a bowl, flake tuna well.
Stir in mayonnaise, lime juice, optional ají amarillo for flavor continuity, and salt/pepper.
Fold in finely diced onion and celery if using.
Taste and adjust: add more lime for brightness or a touch more mayo for creaminess.
checks: Filling is creamy but not runny (should hold shape when layered).
Salt/acid balance is pleasant when chilled.

steps.title: Prepare your mold or shaping method
instructions: Option A (best): Line a ring mold or springform with plastic wrap for easy unmolding.
Option B: Use a loaf pan lined with plastic wrap for a terrine style.
Option C: Free-form: shape individual portions using a small ring mold or by hand.
checks: Mold is lined or lightly oiled so the causa releases cleanly.

steps.title: Layer the causa
instructions: Press half of the potato mixture into the mold as an even base layer.
Add sliced avocado (optional) as a middle layer.
Spread filling evenly over the avocado (or directly over potato).
Top with the remaining potato mixture and smooth the surface.
Cover and chill until firm enough to slice cleanly.
checks: Layers are even and compact (no large gaps).
Top surface is smooth for clean presentation.

steps.title: Garnish and serve chilled
instructions: Unmold onto a plate, remove plastic wrap, and slice into portions.
Garnish with hard-boiled egg and black olives (optional).
Sprinkle chopped herbs if desired. Serve chilled or cool room temperature.

refrigerate.notes: Keep tightly covered. Avocado layer may brown slightly; lime helps slow browning.

freeze.notes: Potato texture becomes watery/grainy when thawed.

notes: Keep chilled (≤40°F / 4°C) for serving and storage.
Do not leave at room temperature for more than 2 hours (1 hour if very warm).

makeAheadNotes: Causa is best after chilling—great for events.
Potato base can be made a few hours ahead; keep covered to prevent drying.
scalingNotes: Double for a 9x13 platter or two loaf pans.
For individual portions, use a 3-inch ring mold and chill briefly before unmolding.

notes: Texture tip: Steam-drying potatoes prevents watery layers and helps the causa slice cleanly.
If potato mix feels too soft, chill 10–15 minutes before layering; it firms as it cools.
If you prefer a richer potato base, add 1–2 tbsp butter, but keep an eye on softness.
Replace tuna with 3 cups shredded cooked chicken.
Add 1 tsp Dijon mustard (optional) for depth.
Use a splash of reserved chicken stock if filling is too thick.

variations.label: Vegetarian (chickpea or hearts of palm filling)

notes: Use 2 cups mashed chickpeas or chopped hearts of palm + diced veggies.
Bind with mayo or a vegan mayo alternative.
Add extra lime and a pinch of cumin for savory depth.
Replace half the mayo with plain Greek yogurt.
Increase salt slightly and add more lime to keep flavor bright.
Increase ají amarillo paste in potato base to 3 tbsp.
Add 1 tbsp ají amarillo to filling and taste.
Serve with ají verde on the side.
Chilled dishes with mayo should be kept cold during service.
If transporting, use an insulated cooler with ice packs.
